this method is to extract the xml string by removing the external tags like Immutable tag, here it finds the index of first '>' bracket adding 1 gives immediate  character which is beginning of xml content and end finds the index of last'<'character which is beginning of closing tag. If start index is less than end index it extracts the substring as xml content

Why we are adding header into parameters. Cant we do it like below :

1. Define the Content-Type Header: Create a ContentTypeHeader in the headers section of components.yml.

components:
  headers:
    ContentTypeHeader:
      description: The Content-Type of the request
      schema:
        type: string
        enum:
          - application/json
          - application/xml
      required: true
      
2. Reference the Header in requestBody: Include the Content-Type header in the requestBody section of your operation.

nodeByDataspaceAndAnchor:
  get:
    description: Get a node with an option to retrieve all the children for a given anchor and dataspace
    tags:
      - cps-data
    summary: Get a node
    operationId: getNodeByDataspaceAndAnchorV2
    parameters:
      - $ref: 'components.yml#/components/parameters/dataspaceNameInPath'
      - $ref: 'components.yml#/components/parameters/anchorNameInPath'
      - $ref: 'components.yml#/components/parameters/xpathInQuery'
      - $ref: 'components.yml#/components/parameters/descendantsInQuery'
    requestBody:
      content:
        application/json:
          schema:
            type: object
          headers:
            Content-Type:
              $ref: 'components.yml#/components/headers/ContentTypeHeader'
    responses:
      '200':
        description: OK
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
            examples:
              dataSample:
                $ref: 'components.yml#/components/examples/dataSample'
          application/xml:
            schema:
              type: object
              xml:
                name: stores
            examples:
              dataSample:
                $ref: 'components.yml#/components/examples/dataSampleXml'
      '400':
        $ref: 'components.yml#/components/responses/BadRequest'
      '403':
        $ref: 'components.yml#/components/responses/Forbidden'
      '500':
        $ref: 'components.yml#/components/responses/InternalServerError'
    x-codegen-request-body-name: xpath

Here transformer Exception and parser Exception ate thrown due to issues that occur during XML processing infrastructure Generally thrown by the XML processing library. dataMaps will not cause TransformerException so any exception that occurs related to invalid XML element names or structures in the dataMaps will throw a DOMException during the DOM creation. TransformerException occurs after the DOM and the Transformer tries to serialize the DOM into an XML output and an issue occurs during the transformation. Suppose if we transform XML to a file or stream, I/O problems (e.g., file permissions, disk space, stream errors) can occur,using invalid or unsupported encoding (e.g., INVALID_ENCODING), XSL transformations with XML and the stylesheet has errors , it will throw a TransformerException. Have seen these exception have been used in YangParserHelper observed that there also there were no testcase written
* @throws RuntimeException if an error occurs during XML conversion
